extraction.one_line_summary: VAN BAARLE Dylan won after INEOS exploited early crosswinds, LAMPAERT crashed late from the lead group, and VAN DER POEL Mathieu failed tactically despite being present
tactical_patterns: GANNA Filippo initiated early echelon split in crosswinds before cobbles, eliminating PEDERSEN Mads via positioning crash
INEOS pre-emptively shelled race with gutter riding before pave sectors, suggesting crosswind reconnaissance overrides cobble-focused preparation
VAN DER POEL Mathieu declined to chase critical moves despite presence, indicating energy reservation backfired against committed riders
LAMPAERT Yves crashed from winning position late, suggesting fatigue-induced handling errors outweigh pure power in finale
TURNER Ben delivered exceptional domestique work despite late crash, enabling VAN BAARLE victory through positioning sacrifice

rider_signals.VAN BAARLE Dylan: positive: transformed from previous year's time-cut elimination to victory, indicating INEOS system refinement
rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: positive: orchestrated decisive early echelon split using crosswinds before pave, showing tactical initiative beyond time-trialing specialty
rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: negative: present in finale but refused to chase moves, tactical passivity cost podium after surviving entire race
rider_signals.LAMPAERT Yves: negative: crashed from leading group late despite superior form, positioning error from fatigue negated strongest ride
rider_signals.KUNG Stefan: positive: third place despite being caught by chase group suggests time-trial power translates when sprint contention reduced
rider_signals.DEVRIENDT Tom: positive: fourth place as complete unknown reveals breakaway survival outweighs reputation in reduced sprint finales
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: negative: crashed before cobbles during echelon formation at 36mph, early positioning error cascaded into chase deficit
rider_signals.STUYVEN Jasper: positive: seventh despite appearing dead final 50km, suggests recovery capacity underestimated in fatigue assessment
rider_signals.TURNER Ben: positive: 10th-11th after critical late domestique work and crash, sacrifice timing maximized leader outcome
rider_signals.SÉNÉCHAL Florian: neutral: top-15 finish as regional rider, local knowledge advantage appears marginal in modern recon era
